content.problemStatement:
Write a program to input two student's names and percentages and print the name of the student with the higher percentage. In case they score equal percentages, print '**equal**'.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two strings: the name of the first student and the name of the second student.
The second line of input will contain two percentages: the percentage of the first student and the percentage of the second student.
content.inputFormat: First line contains two strings denoting the names of the two students. The second line contains two integers denoting their percentages.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the name of the student with the higher percentage. If both percentages are equal, output **equal**.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Only one test case. Amit's percentage (95) is higher than Swarup's (92), so Amit is printed.
Doe's percentage (95) exceeds John's (92), therefore Doe is printed.
Both percentages are equal (95), so the special token **equal** is printed.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by simply comparing the percentages of the two students in each test case and printing the name of the student with the higher percentage. If the percentages are equal, print **equal**.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input.
